author: Doctrinal Treatises of St. Augustin
book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.

ccel.title: NPNF1-03. On the Holy Trinity; Doctrinal Treatises; Moral Treatises

source.title: NPNF1-03. On the Holy Trinity; Doctrinal Treatises; Moral Treatises

title: Of Christ’s Ascension into Heaven.

source.title: NPNF1-03. On the Holy Trinity; Doctrinal Treatises; Moral Treatises

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: We believe that He Ascended into Heaven, which place of blessedness He has likewise promised unto us, saying, “They shall be as the angels in the heavens,” in that city which is the mother of us all, the Jerusalem eternal in the heavens.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: But it is wont to give offense to certain parties, either impious Gentiles or heretics, that we should believe in the assumption of an earthly body into heaven.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: The Gentiles, however, for the most part, set themselves diligently to ply us with the arguments of the philosophers, to the effect of affirming that there cannot possibly be anything earthly in heaven.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: For they know not our Scriptures, neither do they understand how it has been said, “It is sown an animal body, it is raised a spiritual body.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: For thus it has not been expressed, as if body were turned into spirit and became spirit; inasmuch as at present, too, our body, which is called animal (animale), has not been turned into soul and become soul (anima).

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: But by a spiritual body is meant one which has been made subject to spirit in such wise that it is adapted to a heavenly habitation, all frailty and every earthly blemish having been changed and converted into heavenly purity and stability.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: This is the change concerning which the apostle likewise speaks thus: “We shall all rise, but we shall not all be changed.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: And that this change is made not unto the worse, but unto the better, the same [apostle] teaches, when he says, “And we shall be changed.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: But the question as to where and in what manner the Lord’s body is in heaven, is one which it would be altogether over-curious and superfluous to prosecute.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: Only we must believe that it is in heaven.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: For it pertains not to our frailty to investigate the secret things of heaven, but it does pertain to our faith to hold elevated and honorable sentiments on the subject of the dignity of the Lord’s body.
